paragraphs: Eureka originated in Los Angeles, California, selected from seedlings grown from Italian seed and introduced around 1877 as Garey's Eureka. Along with Lisbon it became one of the world's principal lemon varieties.

The tree is largely thornless and strongly everbearing, producing fruit at the ends of long branches, which makes it a popular choice for home gardens. The acidic fruit has a fairly rough, prominently ridged rind and only a small nipple.

claims.text: Originated in Los Angeles, California, from seedlings of Italian seed and introduced around 1877 as Garey's Eureka
Along with Lisbon, became one of the world's principal lemon varieties
Tree is largely thornless and strongly everbearing, fruiting at the ends of long branches
Acidic fruit with a fairly rough, prominently ridged rind and a small nipple

sources.name: Givaudan Citrus Variety Collection (UC Riverside): Frost nucellar Eureka lemon

paragraphs: Lisbon is a lemon of Portuguese origin, believed to be a Gallego seedling selection, and was introduced to Australia early in the 1800s. It became one of the leading commercial lemons in both Australia and the United States.

The tree is vigorous, large, thorny and densely foliated, and is notably resistant to frost, heat, wind and neglect, more so than Eureka. Because much of the fruit is borne within the canopy it is well protected, and the oblong, very acidic fruit carries a prominent nipple.

claims.text: Lemon of Portuguese origin, believed to be a Gallego seedling selection, introduced to Australia early in the 1800s
Became a leading commercial lemon in Australia and the United States
Tree is vigorous, large, thorny and densely foliated, and notably resistant to frost, heat, wind and neglect, more so than Eureka
Much of the fruit is borne within the canopy, providing protection
Oblong, very acidic fruit with a prominent nipple

sources.name: Givaudan Citrus Variety Collection (UC Riverside): Frost nucellar Lisbon lemon

paragraphs: The Meyer lemon is native to China and was introduced to the United States in 1908 by USDA plant explorer Frank Meyer. It is not a true lemon but a hybrid, generally described as a cross of citron with a mandarin/pomelo type.

Its fruit is sweeter and less acidic than standard Eureka or Lisbon lemons, with thin, deep-yellow to faintly orange skin and a subtle floral, almost orange-like character. A virus-free selection was released by the University of California in 1975 as the Improved Meyer lemon.

claims.text: Native to China and introduced to the United States in 1908 by USDA plant explorer Frank Meyer
Not a true lemon but a hybrid, generally a cross of citron with a mandarin/pomelo type
Sweeter and less acidic than Eureka or Lisbon lemons, with a subtle orange-like character
Thin, deep-yellow to faintly orange skin
A virus-free selection was released by the University of California in 1975 as the Improved Meyer lemon
